terms.definition: The year in which income earned during the Financial Year is assessed and taxed. AY 2025-26 is for income earned in FY 2024-25.
The year in which income is earned, running from April 1 to March 31.
The total income from all five heads (Salary, House Property, Business, Capital Gains, Other Sources) before deductions.
Gross Total Income minus eligible deductions under Chapter VIA (80C, 80D, etc.).
Tax deducted by the payer (employer, bank, etc.) before making payment, deposited with the government on behalf of the taxpayer.
A unique 10-character alphanumeric identifier issued by the Income Tax Department for tax tracking purposes.
A TDS certificate issued by the employer showing salary details, TDS deducted, and applicable deductions for the financial year.
Annual Tax Statement showing all taxes deposited against your PAN, including TDS, advance tax, and self-assessment tax.
The form filed with the Income Tax Department declaring income, deductions, and tax liability for a financial year.
A flat deduction from salary income. ₹50,000 under old regime, ₹75,000 under new regime for FY 2024-25.
Additional tax levied on income exceeding ₹50 lakhs. Rates vary from 10% to 37% based on total income.
A 4% cess on total tax plus surcharge, used to fund health and education programs.
Deduction up to ₹1.5 lakh for investments in PPF, ELSS, NSC, life insurance, tuition fees, home loan principal, etc.
Deduction for medical insurance premium paid for self, family, and parents. Limits vary from ₹25,000 to ₹1,00,000.
Additional deduction of ₹50,000 for contribution to National Pension System (NPS), over and above Section 80C limit.
Deduction for interest paid on education loan for higher education. No upper limit. Available for 8 years.
Deduction for donations to approved charitable institutions. 50% or 100% deduction based on the institution.
Allowance received from employer for rent payment. Exempt from tax under specific conditions and formula.
Exemption for travel expenses within India. Available twice in a block of four years.
Deduction for interest on home loan. Max ₹2 lakh for self-occupied property, no limit for let-out property.
Tax rebate for individuals with taxable income up to ₹5 lakh (old) or ₹7 lakh (new regime). Max rebate ₹12,500 (old) or ₹25,000 (new).
Tax paid in installments during the financial year when estimated tax liability exceeds ₹10,000.
Tax paid by the assessee on the balance tax due after considering TDS and advance tax.
Interest penalties for late filing (234A), non-payment of advance tax (234B), and deferment of advance tax (234C).
Profit from selling capital assets. STCG (short-term) and LTCG (long-term) have different tax rates.
Gains from selling assets held for less than the specified period (12 months for equity, 24 months for property).
Gains from selling assets held beyond the specified period. Taxed at special rates (10%/20% depending on asset type).
Adjusting the purchase price of an asset for inflation using the Cost Inflation Index (CII) to reduce capital gains tax.
Simplified taxation for small businesses (Section 44AD/44ADA) where income is presumed at 6-8% of turnover (businesses) or 50% (professionals).
Income from agriculture, which is exempt from income tax but used to determine the applicable tax rate (partial integration).
Non-cash benefits provided by employer (car, accommodation, etc.) that are taxable as part of salary income.
Income not subject to tax, such as agricultural income, certain dividends, PPF maturity, life insurance proceeds under specific conditions.
Mandatory audit of books of accounts by a CA if business turnover exceeds ₹1 crore (₹10 crore for digital transactions) or professional receipts exceed ₹50 lakhs.
ITR filed after the due date but before the end of the assessment year. Subject to penalty and loss of certain benefits.
Corrected ITR filed under Section 139(5) to rectify errors in the original return, before the end of the assessment year.
Mandatory electronic verification of a filed ITR using Aadhaar OTP, net banking, bank account, Demat account, or physical ITR-V.
Comprehensive statement showing all financial transactions reported against your PAN, including salary, interest, dividends, property, and more.
Derived from AIS, showing aggregated tax information used for pre-filling ITR forms.
Tax structure with higher rates but allowing all deductions and exemptions (80C, 80D, HRA, etc.). Optional from FY 2023-24.
Default regime from FY 2023-24 with lower tax rates but very limited deductions. Only standard deduction and employer NPS allowed.
Relief provided when surcharge causes total tax to exceed the additional income above the surcharge threshold.
State-level tax on employment/profession. Maximum ₹2,500 per year. Deductible from salary income.
Lump sum benefit paid by employer upon retirement/resignation. Exempt up to ₹20 lakhs for non-government employees.
Strategy of selling loss-making investments to offset capital gains and reduce tax liability.
Adjusting losses from one head of income against gains from same or other heads. Unadjusted losses can be carried forward up to 8 years.
Treaty between countries to prevent income from being taxed twice. Provides relief through exemption or credit method.
Government-backed retirement savings scheme offering tax benefits under Sections 80CCD(1), 80CCD(1B), and 80CCD(2).
Government savings scheme with 15-year lock-in. Interest and maturity are tax-free (EEE status). Currently ~7.1% return.
Tax-saving mutual funds with 3-year lock-in. Offers market-linked returns with ₹1.5 lakh deduction under 80C.
